message:
fix(x-stats-card): defer state updates in reduced motion handler

Wrap the reduced motion preference state updates in setTimeout(_, 0)
to ensure they execute after the current render cycle completes.
This prevents potential React state update conflicts during the
hydration phase when the component detects that the user prefers
reduced motion.

Changes:
- Wrap setCount and setHasAnimated calls in setTimeout
- Add proper cleanup by returning clearTimeout in useEffect
- Add explicit return undefined for consistent effect cleanup

This follows the same pattern applied to stats-grid.tsx and
market-cap-drop.tsx for SSR hydration safety.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
